zh.Eyton near Wem, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾艾頓 亞爾 維姆
zh.Eyton upon the Weald Moors, Shropshire,: 英國史洛普爾艾頓 武朋 馮 維亞爾德 波斯
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Jeanette de Eyton於1347年出生在英國史洛普爾艾頓 亞爾 維姆。她於1378年與Humphrey De Eyton在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1451年在她的家鄉享年104歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Jeanette de Eyton于1347年出生在英国史洛普爾艾頓 亞爾 維姆。她于1378年与Humphrey De Eyton在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1451年在她的家乡享年104岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Eyton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Eyton (Shrops) or Eaton (Cheshire, Leics, Staffs), or Eaton Bray (Beds), Cold Eaton or Long Eaton (Derbys).

variants.name: Ayton
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Ayton (North Yorkshire) or Ayton (Berwickshire). The former is derived from Old Norse á ‘river, stream’ + tūn ‘farmstead’. The later takes its name from the River Eye (likely from Old English ēa ‘river, stream’) + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanette
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jeannette  , in the 1920s and 30s associated particularly with the Americam singer and film star  Jeanette MacDonald  (  1902–65 ).

variants.name: Jeannette
meanings: diminutive form of Jeanne, feminine form of Jean ‘John’, now also used in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Jeanette de Eyton was born in 1347, in Eyton near Wem, Shropshire, England. She married Humphrey De Eyton in 1378, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1451, in her hometown, at the age of 104.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Jeanette d’Eyton est née en 1347, à Eyton near Wem, Shropshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Humphrey De Eyton en 1378, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1451, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 104 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Jeanette de Eytonは1347年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアエイトン ネアル ウエムで生まれた。1378年にイギリスでHumphrey De Eytonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1451年に故郷で104歳で死去した。

ko.Eyton upon the Weald Moors, Shropshire,: 영국 슈럽실르 이턴 업 테 위드 무르스
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jeanette de Eyton(은)는 1347년 영국 슈럽실르 이턴 니아어 w에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1378년 영국에서 Humphrey De Eyton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1451년 그녀의 고향에서 104세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Jeanette de Eyton родилась в 1347 г. в местности эитон нир вем, шропшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Humphrey De Eyton в 1378 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1451 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 104 лет.
